# RQM Docs Agent Guide (Concise)

- site_identity: RQM Platform documentation (MkDocs Material)
- canonical_docs_url: https://docs.rqmtechnologies.com/
- api_base_url: https://rqm-api.onrender.com
- swagger_url: https://rqm-api.onrender.com/docs

## Key docs

- Agent Portal: /agents/
- Prompt Pack: /agents/prompts/
- Cursor / Codex Setup: /agents/cursor/
- API Overview: /api/overview/
- Quickstart: /getting-started/quickstart/
- Platform Overview: /platform/overview/
- Canonical Circuit Boundary: /platform/canonical-circuit-boundary/
- Optimization Engine: /platform/optimization-engine/
- Execution Bridges: /platform/execution-bridges/
- Verification / Trust: /products/verification-trust/

## Core capabilities

- Circuit validation, analysis, and optimization via `rqm-api`
- Execution routing through explicit bridges (Qiskit/IBM, Braket, PennyLane)
- Quaternionic/SU(2) math utilities and geometry-native analysis across platform components
- RQM Studio workflow layer for browser-based orchestration

## Agent rules

1. Read this file first.
2. Use documented API routes and Swagger UI.
3. Validate circuit payloads before optimization.
4. Use public circuit boundary payloads; do not assume internal IR contracts.
5. Do not invent undocumented endpoints/fields.
6. Label research concepts as research.
7. Keep claims conservative; do not claim physical advantage without benchmark or output artifact evidence.
8. Generate reproducible artifacts where possible.

## Production vs research

- Production/documented: public API boundary, schema-guided payload exchange, trust posture, execution bridge model.
- Research: speculative workflows, unshipped MCP tools, or unverified performance/advantage claims.

## Recommended first steps

1. Read `/llms.txt` and `/api/overview/`.
2. Confirm routes in Swagger UI.
3. Start from documented example payloads.
4. Implement validate -> analyze/optimize -> execution flow.
